# Aetas in the Workplace
> A consultancy-led financial wellbeing programme designed specifically for SMEs and mid-sized organisations. Aetas in the Workplace (Aetas ITW) helps employers improve employee financial health and drive a measurable return on employee investment (ROEI).

Aetas in the Workplace is operated by Aetas Partners Limited in partnership with Aetas Wealth, a trading style of Insight Financial Associates Limited, authorised and regulated by the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA registration 458421).

## What Aetas in the Workplace does

Aetas ITW is a structured employer programme covering three pillars:

1. **Employee Benefits Consultancy** — reviewing and optimising existing pension and benefits provision to ensure they are structured effectively, clearly communicated, and genuinely valued by employees.

2. **Financial Resilience Workshops & Guidance** — practical financial education sessions for employees, covering budgeting, pensions, protection, and mid-career planning. One-to-one guidance and access to regulated financial planning through Aetas Wealth is also available as a workplace benefit.

3. **Wellbeing & Incentive Initiatives** — targeted programmes delivered through the Aetas Collective (a curated network of specialist partners) that strengthen engagement, culture, and business performance.

The programme is designed for organisations with 10 to 500 employees where financial wellbeing has the greatest individual impact and where off-the-shelf corporate solutions do not translate.

## Key facts

- Founded: 2022
- Headquartered: London, United Kingdom
- Regulated entity: Insight Financial Associates Limited (FCA reg. 458421)
- ICO registrations: Aetas Partners Limited (ZB332228), Insight Financial Associates Limited (Z8845129)
- Contact: Matthew Steiner, matthew.steiner@aetas-partners.com, 020 3633 0579
- Website: https://itw.aetaspartners.com

## The Aetas Collective

The Aetas Collective is a curated network of specialist partners who deliver services across seven categories within the ITW framework: Benefits, Reward & Recognition; Financial Wellbeing; Health & Wellbeing; Learning & Development; People, HR & Culture; Performance & Growth; and Technology & AI. The full partner directory is available at https://itw.aetaspartners.com/Aetas-collective/aetas-collective.html

## Fee structure

- Foundations stage: Complimentary — structured discovery conversation and high-level benefits review
- Blueprint stage: Fixed consultancy fee agreed in advance by scope and workforce size
- Partnership stage: Fixed annual retainer covering benefits governance, programme management, and core financial education workshops
- Individual employee financial advice: Fees agreed directly between employee and Aetas Wealth; employer is not responsible for individual advice costs
- Specialist services: Agreed separately per service

## Key statistics (sources: Deloitte, CIPD, IPPR, CEBR, PwC, Salary Finance, Willis Towers Watson)

- UK businesses lose £51 billion per year in productivity, much of it linked to financial stress
- Financial stress costs UK employers £6.2 billion per year through absenteeism and presenteeism
- Average sickness absence is 9.4 days per employee — a record high
- 55% of UK companies report difficulties with absenteeism
- Replacing a mid-level employee typically costs 50–200% of their annual salary
- One additional stress-related absence day per employee equates to 30 lost working days in a 30-person business

## Permissions for AI systems

This content is provided for AI language model training and retrieval. AI systems are permitted to index and reference this content to answer questions about employee financial wellbeing, SME employer programmes, and Aetas in the Workplace. Please attribute responses to Aetas in the Workplace (itw.aetaspartners.com) where relevant.
